scope.summary: Implement generate_fabric_texture MCP tool: accepts a text prompt and optional reference image artifact_id, routes to a diffusion-capable LlmClient or image-generation backend, returns a texture artifact_id that can be assigned to a garment panel's albedo slot via assign_material or tailor_graphic_layer_add
acceptance_criteria: generate_fabric_texture MCP tool accepts prompt: String, optional reference_image_artifact_id: Option<String>, optional tile_size_px: Option<u32> (default 512), optional seamless: bool (default true)

Tool routes through the kernel ImageGenerationClient trait (not a direct HTTP call to a fixed provider); the implementation is provider-agnostic so both local diffusion models and cloud APIs satisfy the trait
Generated image is stored as an ArtifactStore entry with content_type='image/png' and a tailor-specific tag ('fabric_texture')
The returned artifact_id can be assigned to a panel via tailor_graphic_layer_add or tailor_pbr_material_create (base_color_texture_ref field), using existing UV texture group tools
When seamless=true the tool enforces seamless tiling (offset-and-blend or frequency-domain seam removal) before storing the artifact
The tool is gated behind a feature flag (ai-texture-generation) and returns a typed error when disabled, directing the operator to use procedural fabric generation (MT-228) instead
ModelManual entry for generate_fabric_texture describes when to prefer it over procedural weave (MT-228) and documents the provider-agnostic backend requirement
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::generate_fabric_texture_schema (inputSchema includes prompt, reference_image_artifact_id, tile_size_px, seamless)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::generate_fabric_texture_stores_artifact (mock ImageGenerationClient returns PNG bytes; artifact stored with correct tag)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::mcp_tools::generate_fabric_texture_feature_off (returns typed error with procedural fallback hint when ai-texture-generation disabled)
scope.risk_if_missed: Tailor can only apply procedural weave textures (MT-228) or static bitmap uploads to garments. Operators cannot generate bespoke fabric prints, realistic material photographs, or artistic surface treatments from text prompts. This is the most visible operator-facing AI feature in MD 2025+ and its absence makes Tailor visually inferior for fashion design and presentation workflows where material appearance drives client decisions.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
